feedback.text: I feel like the digital fairness act is already on the right track to protect the consumer rights of digital goods, but there are a few important things missing right now. 1. Publishers of video games have been destroying our video games for years and it is only getting worse by the day. They want us to feel comfortable about it now too (quote from ubisoft). I want to be able to continue playing the games I bought even if the publishers decide to sunset the game. An end of life plan would be the best and would not force them to keep their servers running as they can just let us run local servers instead. Currently, from the 1038 documented games, only 23.6% has been preserved, whilst 34.49% can no longer be played and 37.48% is at risk of being killed as they do not have an end of life plan. The remaining 4.43% does not fall in to any of these categories. I ask to protect our video games from being destroyed by the publishers so we can continue playing the games we paid for! 2. Recently, a new threat has emerged. Payment processors with monopoly positions such as PayPal, Visa and Mastercard have begun censoring major software distribution platforms such as Steam and itch.io. They have forced their hand with their monopoly position to remove games they did not like (ones that are legal). If the software distribution platforms would not agree, they would no longer be able to use PayPal, Visa and Mastercard as purchasing options for their platforms. This should not be allowed as they will control our entire space soon, removing anything they dont like with nothing we can do about it as they have a monopoly in their field.
